skill.name: todoist-automation
skill.description: Automate Todoist task management, projects, sections, filtering, and bulk operations via Rube MCP (Composio).
skill.summary: Automate Todoist workflows including task creation, project organization, and bulk operations through Rube MCP's Todoist toolkit.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is documentation-only (markdown file describing Todoist automation workflows). All 233 static findings for 'external_commands' are false positives - they are markdown code blocks, not executable Ruby/shell code. The network finding is a documented MCP endpoint for user configuration. Weak crypto findings are alphanumeric Todoist API IDs in documentation. Safe for publication with minor network disclosure warning.

low_findings.title: External MCP Server Endpoint
low_findings.description: Documentation references external MCP server URL (https://rube.app/mcp) that users must add to their configuration. This is a legitimate third-party service dependency.

content.value_statement: Streamline your Todoist productivity by automating repetitive task operations through AI. Create, organize, and manage tasks, projects, and sections without manual clicking through the Todoist interface.
actual_capabilities: Create, update, complete, reopen, and delete Todoist tasks via MCP tools
Manage projects and sections with full CRUD operations
Search and filter tasks using Todoist's native filter syntax
Bulk create multiple tasks in a single API request
Retrieve completed task history by completion date range
limitations: Requires active Rube MCP connection and Todoist OAuth authorization
Cannot execute operations without valid Todoist API connection
Filter queries must reference actual existing projects, labels, and entities in user's account
Completed task retrieval limited to approximately 3 months per query

use_cases.title: Project Scaffolding
use_cases.description: Rapidly create a new project with all sections and initial tasks populated for a structured workflow.

use_cases.title: Recurring Task Setup
use_cases.description: Configure recurring tasks with natural language scheduling for habits and regular responsibilities.

use_cases.title: Task Cleanup and Reorganization
use_cases.description: Bulk complete, move, or reorganize tasks across projects and sections for inbox zero maintenance.
use_cases.target_user: Power users maintaining organized task hierarchies

prompt_templates.title: Basic Task Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a task in Todoist called 'Review quarterly report' with high priority due next Friday at 2pm in the Work project.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick single task creation with due date and priority

prompt_templates.title: Project Setup with Sections
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new project called 'Website Redesign' with sections for Design, Development, and Testing. Add three initial tasks to each section with appropriate priorities.
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up a structured project from scratch

prompt_templates.title: Bulk Task Import
prompt_templates.prompt: I have 15 tasks for my conference planning checklist. Create them all at once in the 'Conference 2024' project, distributed across Logistics, Speakers, and Marketing sections with varying priorities.
prompt_templates.scenario: Efficiently importing a prepared task list

prompt_templates.title: Task Search and Batch Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all overdue tasks tagged with @urgent and update them to have tomorrow as the due date with highest priority.

output_examples.input: Create a task for team meeting every Monday at 10am
output_examples.output: Created recurring task 'team meeting' in Inbox with schedule: every Monday at 10:00 AM, priority: normal

output_examples.input: Set up a Home Renovation project with Budget, Contractors, and Timeline sections
output_examples.output: Created project 'Home Renovation' with 3 sections: Budget, Contractors, Timeline. Project is ready for task addition.

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before executing workflows
Verify connection status is ACTIVE before running any Todoist operations
Use bulk create operations instead of sequential single-task creation to reduce API calls
anti_patterns: Embedding due dates in task content or description instead of using dedicated due_string field
Using filter syntax with non-existent project names, labels, or entities causing API errors
Attempting to use completed task filters on GET_ALL_TASKS endpoint which only returns incomplete tasks

faq.question: Do I need a Todoist API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No API key is needed directly. The skill uses Rube MCP which handles Todoist OAuth authentication. You authorize once through Rube's connection management and the skill uses that authenticated connection.

faq.question: What is Rube MCP and how do I set it up?
faq.answer: Rube MCP is an MCP server that provides access to various integrations including Todoist. Add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server in your AI client configuration, then use RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS to authorize Todoist access.

faq.question: Can I create recurring tasks with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. Use natural language in the due_string parameter like 'every Monday at 9am' or 'every Friday' to create recurring tasks. Todoist will automatically generate recurring instances.

faq.question: Why does priority 4 mean urgent when the UI shows p1 as urgent?
faq.answer: The Todoist API uses inverted priority numbering compared to the UI. API priority 1 is normal, 4 is urgent. The UI displays p1 as urgent visually. This skill uses API conventions, so use 4 for urgent tasks.

faq.question: Can I retrieve tasks I already completed?
faq.answer: Yes, but you must use the GET_COMPLETED_TASKS_BY_COMPLETION_DATE endpoint with a date range. The regular GET_ALL_TASKS endpoint only returns incomplete tasks. Date range queries are limited to approximately 3 months.

faq.question: What happens if I use a filter with a project that doesn't exist?
faq.answer: The Todoist API will return an HTTP 400 error. Filter syntax requires referencing actual existing entities in your account. Always list projects or labels first to verify names before using them in filters.
